next_steps: Urinalysis to check for hematuria or proteinuria
Complete blood count with differential and inflammatory markers (CRP and ESR)
Echocardiogram to evaluate for coronary artery involvement
Blood cultures and consideration of empirical IV antibiotics
Serum creatinine and BUN to assess renal function
Urgent blood cultures and administration of broad-spectrum intravenous antibiotics (e.g., ceftriaxone and vancomycin) until sepsis/meningococcemia is ruled out
Complete blood count with differential and platelet count to assess for thrombocytopenia and leukocytosis
Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) and coagulation studies (PT, PTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer)
Urinalysis to screen for hematuria or proteinuria
Echocardiogram to evaluate for coronary artery abnormalities associated with Kawasaki disease or MIS-C
Viral testing (COVID-19 PCR, respiratory pathogen panel) and streptococcal throat swab
Skin biopsy of a representative purpuric lesion for histological and direct immunofluorescence studies
